channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Japan's foreign minister seeks "positive" direction in bilateral ties during his visit to China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Japanese Foreign Minister Takeshi Iwaya met with top Chinese leaders Wednesday during his visit to Beijing, the first since the heads of the two countries met at a summit in Peru last month, in an effort to make positive inroads in the bilateral relationship.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu wishes a merry Christmas to Christians around the world
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu wished Christians worldwide a Merry Christmas and thanked the Christian community for its support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — In a dim one-room apartment in one of Istanbul’s poorest neighborhoods, 11-year-old Atakan Sahin curls up on a threadbare sofa with his siblings to watch TV while their mother stirs a pot of pasta. The simple meal is all the family of six can look forward to most evenings.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian ship sinks in Mediterranean after explosion
item.description: A Russian cargo ship sank in the Mediterranean Sea on Monday leaving two of the 16 crew members unaccounted for according to the country’s authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: After 20 years, the post-tsunami generation stays vigilant for future disasters
item.description: LHOKNGA, Indonesia (AP) — Qurrata Ayuni, a 28-year-old survivor of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated her hometown two decades ago, has transformed her resilience into purpose.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, ACHMAD IBRAHIM, and FADLAN SYAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Laredo getting $20M in federal funds to help ailing water infrastructure system
item.description: The border city of Laredo, Texas, is getting an infusion of $20 million to help its troubled water infrastructure system, U.S. Rep. Henry Cuellar announced.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Haiti gang attack on the reopening of a main hospital kills 2 reporters and a police officer
item.description: Haiti’s online media association says two reporters were killed and several were wounded in a gang attack on the reopening of Port-au-Prince’s biggest public hospital.
item.dc:creator: Evens Sanon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian court sentences US citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage
item.description: A Russian court has sentenced a U.S. citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage on Tuesday, according to Russian state media outlets. Eugene Spector, who was born in Russia but later moved to the U.S. and got his U.S. citizenship, was already serving a 3 1/2-year sentence for bribery.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: France has a new government, again. Politics and crushing debt complicate next steps
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s president and prime minister managed to form a new government just in time for the holidays. Now comes the hard part.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US and UK criticize Pakistani military court convictions of civilian supporters of Imran Khan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The United States and the United Kingdom have expressed concern over convictions imposed by Pakistani military courts to 25 civilian supporters of former Prime Minister Imran Khan over their alleged involvement in riots last year.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News's Kilmeade estimates Greenland could cost $1.5 trillion
item.description: Fox News’s Brian Kilmeade estimated Monday that Greenland could cost some $1.5 trillion, after President-elect Trump said earlier this week the U.S. owning the country “is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Baby mammoth preserved for 50,000 years is unveiled in Russia's Siberia
item.description: The 50,000-year-old remains of a baby mammoth uncovered by melting permafrost have been unveiled to the public by researchers in Russia’s Siberia region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greenland to Trump: We're not for sale
item.description: Greenland’s prime minister said Monday the autonomous island is not for sale after President-elect Trump said owning Greenland “is an absolute necessity” for the U.S. “Greenland is ours.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Magdeburg mourns Christmas market attack victims as fears swirl of deeper German social divisions
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Mourners laid flowers near the scene of the deadly Christmas market attack on Monday as investigators puzzled over the motive of the suspect and his previous encounters with authorities were scrutinized, while fears swirled that the rampage could deepen divisions in German
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Holy Year is about to start in Rome. Here's what you need to know
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis on Tuesday formally inaugurates the 2025 Holy Year, reviving an ancient church tradition encouraging the faithful to make pilgrimages to Rome, amid new security fears following a Christmas market attack in Germany.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia approves extradition of former US Marine over alleged training of Chinese military pilots
item.description: NEWCASTLE, Australia (AP) — Former U.S. Marine Corps pilot Daniel Duggan will be extradited from Australia to the United States over allegations that he illegally trained Chinese aviators.
item.dc:creator: BY KEIRAN SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia's Putin holds talks with Slovakian PM Fico, in a rare visit to Moscow by an EU leader
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Sunday hosted Slovakia’s prime minister, Robert Fico, in a rare visit to the Kremlin by an EU leader since Moscow's all-out invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Christmas in Puerto Rico is a 45-day celebration with caroling, festive decorations, family feasts and more
item.description: Christmas in Puerto Rico is spent with loved ones singing carols, sitting around the table for a meal and exchanging gifts. There celebrations last from Thanksgiving to mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least 22 as Vatican envoy visits Christians for pre-Christmas Mass
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday killed at least 22 people, including five children, Palestinian medical officials said, while Gaza's small Christian community celebrated a pre-Christmas Mass.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: LIVE Tracker: Santa Claus is en route in his flight across the world to deliver gifts
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 dead in Brazil bus and truck collision
item.description: Over 30 people were killed after a packed bus collided with a truck in Brazil; the other passengers were transported to a local hospital and remain in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: Over 10 years after flight MH370 is believed to have crashed in the Indian Ocean, Malaysia's government has agreed to accept a "no find, no fee" proposal from a U.S. company to resume the hunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran expands weaponization capabilities critical for employing nuclear bomb
item.description: A new report presented by the National Council of Resistance of Iran claims Tehran has expanded attempts to secretly develop weaponization capabilities for its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
